title: B.Sc. (Hons with Research) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Biology and Serology
content: The B.Sc. (Hons. with Research) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Biology and Serology is a research-driven undergraduate program that offers an in-depth exploration of biological evidence analysis in forensic investigations. The program blends comprehensive theoretical instruction with hands-on training in advanced laboratories dedicated to DNA profiling, bloodstain pattern analysis, body fluid identification, and tissue examination. Students gain practical experience through lab-based learning, mock crime scene investigations, and independent research projects, fostering analytical thinking and scientific precision. The curriculum integrates foundational forensic science with specialized biological techniques, ensuring graduates are equipped to handle complex biological evidence in real-world scenarios. This specialization opens diverse career opportunities in forensic science laboratories, law enforcement agencies, medical examiner’s offices, hospitals, research institutes, and biotechnology firms. The research component of the program prepares students for postgraduate studies and careers in forensic biology, molecular diagnostics, and biomedical research. As biological evidence continues to play a vital role in modern forensic investigations and legal processes, this program offers a strong platform for students aiming to contribute to justice and public safety through scientific expertise and innovation. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 Build a strong foundation in forensic science with a focus on biological and serological techniques used in crime scene investigation and evidence analysis. 02 Develop practical skills in the collection, preservation, and examination of biological evidence such as blood, hair, tissues, and bodily fluids. 03 Train students in advanced laboratory methods including DNA analysis, blood typing, and serological testing for accurate identification and profiling. 04 Cultivate critical thinking and ethical awareness for applying forensic biological data in legal and investigative settings. 05 Promote research and innovation in forensic biology and serology to address emerging challenges in forensic investigations. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 02 03 Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
